info.name: SENTINAL — Complete API + Attack Test Collection
info.description:
Production-verified Postman collection for all SENTINAL services.

Services covered:

- Gateway API (:3000)
    
- Detection Engine (:8002)
    
- PCAP Processor (:8003)
    
- Nexus Agent (:8004)
    

Includes: health checks, normal flows, and controlled attack simulations for each service.

Base URL variable: {{base}} = [http://98.82.8.144:3000](http://98.82.8.144:3000)  
Set in Postman Environment.

item.name: 00 — Health Checks (All Services)

exec: pm.test('Status ok', () => { const b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b.status).to.eql('ok'); });
pm.test('OpenClaw loaded', () => { const b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b.openclaw_loaded).to.be.true; });

item.description: Verify all 4 backend services are live before running any tests.

item.name: 01 — Gateway API — Normal Flows

exec: pm.test('Data is array', () => { const d = pm.response.json().data; pm.expect(d).to.be.an('array'); });
// Save first attack ID for forensics test

item.description: Standard Gateway API requests: stats, logs, attacks, alerts, audit.

item.name: 02 — Gateway API — Attack Simulations
[ATTACK] Clean Request — Should NOT Trigger Detection

exec: pm.test('No attack created for clean request', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().success).to.be.true);

item.description: Controlled attack payloads sent to /api/logs/ingest. Each triggers the full pipeline: Detection → Nexus → Dashboard.

item.name: 03 — Nexus Agent — Direct Enforcement Tests
[BLOCK] SQLi Critical — All Dangerous Actions Blocked

item.description:
Test Nexus's OpenClaw enforcement engine directly on :8004.
ALLOW tests: medium/low severity → safe actions auto-execute.
BLOCK tests: critical/high severity → dangerous actions queued.

item.name: 04 — Gateway API — Nexus Trigger (Full Pipeline)
[TRIGGER] Brute Force Critical — BLOCK + Action Queue

item.description:
Use /api/Nexus/trigger to fire the full attack pipeline through the Gateway.
This creates AttackEvent + Alert + calls Nexus — all 4 socket events fire.

item.name: 05 — Action Queue — Human Approval Workflow
item.description:
After firing a critical attack trigger, use these requests to approve or reject blocked actions.

Workflow:
1. Run [TRIGGER] Brute Force Critical from folder 04
2. GET /api/actions/pending — copy an action ID
3. POST approve or reject with that ID

item.name: 06 — Detection Engine — Direct Tests
item.description:
Send payloads directly to the Detection Engine on :8002.
Bypasses the Gateway — useful for testing detection logic in isolation.

item.name: 07 — PCAP Processor — Direct Tests
item.description:
Test PCAP Processor on :8003 directly.
Note: POST /process requires a real filepath on the server.
Health check always works.

item.name: 08 — End-to-End Demo Sequence

exec: console.log('>>> Watch /action-queue — blocked actions should appear NOW');
console.log('>>> Watch /audit — HUMAN_OVERRIDE entry should appear NOW');
console.log('Total Attacks:', d.totalAttacks, '| Total Logs:', d.totalLogs);

item.description:
Run this folder IN ORDER for the full 8-minute live demo.

Sequence:
1. Health Check All
2. SQLi Attack → watch /attacks page
3. XSS Attack → watch /alerts page
4. Brute Force CRITICAL Trigger → watch /action-queue
5. Approve Action → watch /audit for HUMAN_OVERRIDE
6. Check final stats
